label: Qdrant
description: Connect to a Qdrant vector database cluster — self-hosted or Qdrant Cloud — to store and search high-dimensional embeddings.

form.title: Connect Qdrant
form.subtitle: Paste your Qdrant cluster URL and API key to start querying vector collections.

methods.label: Cluster URL + API Key
methods.description: Paste your Qdrant cluster endpoint and API key. Works for both Qdrant Cloud managed clusters and self-hosted instances with API key auth enabled.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Cluster URL and API Key

### Qdrant Cloud (managed)

1. Log in at [cloud.qdrant.io](https://cloud.qdrant.io).
2. Open your cluster from the **Clusters** list.
3. Under **Connection details**, copy the **Cluster URL** (e.g. `https://xyz.us-east-1-0.aws.cloud.qdrant.io:6333`).
4. Go to **Access → API Keys → Create API Key**. Give it a name like `Anton`.
5. Copy the key — it is shown **once** only. Store it safely before closing the dialog.
6. Paste the URL and API key into the fields below.

### Self-hosted Qdrant

1. Ensure your Qdrant instance is running and reachable (default port `6333`).
2. If you started Qdrant with `--api-key` or set `service.api_key` in `config.yaml`, use that value as your API key.
3. If no API key is configured, leave the **API Key** field blank — Anton will connect without authentication.
4. Enter the full URL to your instance (e.g. `http://localhost:6333` or `https://qdrant.mycompany.com`).

### Notes

- The API key is sent as the `api-key` header on every request.
- For TLS-terminated clusters, always use `https://` to avoid sending the key in plaintext.
- You can scope permissions by creating read-only API keys in Qdrant Cloud if Anton only needs search access.

fields.name: cluster_url
fields.label: Cluster URL
fields.description: Full URL of your Qdrant cluster, including protocol and port. Use http://localhost:6333 for a local instance.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: API key for authentication. Required for Qdrant Cloud clusters; optional for self-hosted instances without auth enabled.

fields.name: collection_name
fields.label: Default Collection
fields.description: Optional default collection Anton will read from and write to. Can be overridden per-query.

fields.name: tls_skip_verify
fields.label: Skip TLS verification
fields.description: Disable TLS certificate verification. Enable only for self-hosted clusters with self-signed certificates.
